title: Heatwave, heavy rains likely across parts of T’gana: IMD
search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds") Telangana
full_text:
Hyderabad: Just days after the southwest monsoon entered
Telangana on Monday, the state is witnessing a fresh spell of intense heat, with dry northwesterly winds triggering heatwave conditions across several districts.
Temperature data on Thursday showed Mancherial recording the highest maximum temperature at 42.8 degrees Celsius, followed by Komuram Bheem at 42.7 degrees Celsius, Jayashankar Bhupalpally at 42.4 degrees Celsius, Peddapalli at 42.3 degrees Celsius, Adilabad at 42.2 degrees Celsius and Mulugu at 42.1 degrees Celsius. Hyderabad remained relatively cooler at 37 degrees Celsius, while Rangareddy and Medchal-Malkajgiri recorded 37.2 degrees Celsius and 37.5 degrees Celsius, respectively.
heatwave HOT WEATHER IN DELHI ON SUNDAY.
PHOTO BY RAJESH MEHTA.
The India Meteorological Department has warned that heatwave conditions are very likely to prevail at isolated places in Adilabad, Komuram Bheem Asifabad, Mancherial, Nirmal, Nizamabad, Jagtial, Karimnagar and Peddapalli districts over the next 24 hours.
However, the weather is expected to remain highly contrasting across the state, with heavy rainfall likely at isolated places in Mahabubabad, Warangal, Hanumakonda, Mahabubnagar and Nagarkurnool districts, while thunderstorms accompanied by lightning and gusty winds of 40–50 kmph are expected across Hyderabad and several other districts, indicating unstable monsoon conditions despite the ongoing heatwave.

title: Despite monsoon setting in, heatwave conditions forecast to persist in Telangana till weekend
search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds") Telangana
full_text:
The southwest monsoon has covered parts of Telangana. Yet, the heatwave conditions are forecast to persist till the weekend. Hot conditions continued in a few districts as the highest temperature on Wednesday (June 10, 2026) was 43°C and 18 districts recorded maximum temperature exceeding 42°C.
India Meteorological Department (IMD)-Hyderabad Centre on Thursday (June 11) forecast heatwave conditions as very likely in isolated pockets in seven north Telangana districts for Friday (June 12). Besides this, thunderstorms accompanied with lightning and gusty winds are predicted in 18 districts.
Heatwave in seven Telangana districts
The heatwave conditions are very likely to prevail to be observed in isolated pockets in Adilabad, Kumaram bheem Asifabad, Mancherial, Nirmal, Nizamabad, Karimnagar and Peddapalli districts.
Thunderstorm forecast for 18 Telangana districts
Thunderstorm accompanied with lightning and gusty winds (40-50 kmph) are very likely to occur on Friday (June 12) at isolated places in Nalgonda, Suryapet, Mahabubabad, Warangal, Hanumakonda, Jangaon, Siddipet, Yadadri Bhuvanagiri, Rangareddy, Hyderabad, Medchal Malkajgiri, Vikarabad, Sangareddy, Mahabubnagar, Nagarkurnool, Wanaparthy, Narayanpet and Jogulamba Gadwal districts, as per IMD’s bulletin issued on Thursday.
Besides, heavy rainfall is very likely to occur at isolated places in Nalgonda, Suryapet, Mahabubabad and Nagarkurnool districts.
Maximum temperatures in Telangana on June 10
On Wednesday, the highest of 43°C was recorded in Vemanpally of Mancherial, 42.9°C in Laxmidevipalli of Bhadradri Kothagudem and 42.8°C in Buggaram of Jagtial, as per Telangana Development Planning Society’s data. The maximum temperature was below 40°C only in nine districts.
Published - June 11, 2026 05:09 pm IST

title: Monsoon yet to cover Telangana, IMD issues heatwave alert till June 13 - NewsMeter
full_text:
Monsoon yet to cover Telangana, IMD issues heatwave alert till June 13
The India Meteorological Department has issued a yellow alert for multiple districts, which comes in mid-June, typically the onset period for Southwest Monsoon, yet to cover the entire state.
By - Newsmeter Network |Hyderabad: Just days after scattered rains brought brief relief, Telangana is bracing for another spell of heatwave conditions.
The India Meteorological Department has issued a yellow alert for multiple districts, which comes in mid-June, typically the onset period for Southwest Monsoon, yet to cover the entire state.
Heatwave alert in North Telangana
According to the IMD’s Special Bulletin for Heat Wave issued on Thursday, June 11, heatwave conditions are very likely at isolated pockets in Adilabad, Komuram Bheem Asifabad, Mancherial, Nirmal, Nizamabad, Karimnagar and Peddapalli on June 11.
The alert extends to Jagtial on June 12 and to Jayashankar Bhupalpally on June 13.
Maximum temperatures in these districts are forecast between 41°C and 44°C till June 13. Temperatures are expected to drop to 36-40°C from June 14.
The rest of Telangana, including Hyderabad, is forecast to record 36-40°C till June 15.
Rain and thunderstorms in south, central districts
Even as heat builds in the north, IMD has warned of heavy rainfall at isolated places in Nalgonda, Suryapet, Mahabubabad and Nagarkurnool on June 11. Heavy rain is also likely in Mahabubabad, Warangal, Hanumakonda, Mahabubnagar and Nagarkurnool on June 12.
Thunderstorms with lightning and gusty winds of 40-50 kmph are forecast across most districts, including Hyderabad, Rangareddy, Medchal-Malkajgiri, Warangal and Khammam, till June 17.
Monsoon delay, yellow alert advisory
IMD data indicates the monsoon is yet to cover the whole of Telangana, prolonging hot conditions in the northern belt.
A yellow alert signals ‘be updated’ as moderate temperatures are tolerable for the public but pose a health concern for infants, elderly and people with chronic diseases. The IMD advises avoiding heat exposure, wearing lightweight cotton clothes, and covering the head.
The heatwave warning is valid from June 11 to June 15.
